timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Russia-Ukraine war coverage (per user instruction). Primary source for entries Feb 21, 2022 through Jan 13, 2023 is the AJ longform 'Mapping the major battles of the Ukraine war, one year on' (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/longform/2023/2/21/mapping-the-major-battles-of-the-ukraine-war) which is structured as a chronological battle-by-battle account and supplied 16 specific dated events. Additional contributing articles: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/2/28/russia-ukraine-crisis-in-maps-and-charts-live-news-interactive ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/2/20/eastern-ukraine-front-line-civilians-shelling-russia ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/1/13/russia-says-its-forces-have-taken-control-of-ukraines-soledar ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/25/reporters-notebook-a-traumatised-father-on-ukraines-front-line ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2023/10/27/dispatches-from-ukraines-front-lines-a-strike-devastates-a-village-of-300 ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/3/18/ukraine-debates-mobilising-more-men-to-fight-russia-after-two-years-of-war (Mar 2024 debate article, used to anchor Apr 11 2024 mobilisation-law signing which AJ then covered separately). Articles considered but dropped per rule 5 (out of cluster window): https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/2/6/borderlands-fear-uncertainty-and-life-along-ukraines-frontline (Feb 6, 2022 — predates cluster window) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/17/russia-ukraine-war-list-of-key-events-day-1423 and https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/18/russia-ukraine-war-list-of-key-events-day-1455 (both 2026 day-by-day update articles describing 2026 events, well outside the 2022-2024 cluster window). Date approximations per rule 4: Mariupol fall (May 20, 2022) — the mapping article says 'mid-May' for the Azovstal surrender of ~1,700 Ukrainian defenders; May 20 is the most defensible specific date pegged to the broadly-cited fall of the city, when the last commanders surrendered. Apr 3, 2022 (Battle of Kyiv won) — mapping piece says 'By April 3, Ukraine had won the battle for Kyiv'; used directly. Source disagreements: Mariupol theatre death toll — Ukrainian officials said 300+, later reporting suggested higher; AJ entry preserves the contemporaneously reported figure per rule. Moskva sinking cause — Ukraine claimed missile strike, Russia said internal fire; AJ entry not included on AJ side to avoid duplicating CNN's framing; CNN's timeline carries the event. Bucha — AJ uses ICC prosecutor language 'crime scene' while Russia called killings staged; AJ entry not included separately to avoid the political dispute issue (CNN's timeline covers it via the Apr 1 entry). Prigozhin death (Aug 23, 2023) — AJ reported Russian official confirmation; alternative explanations (assassination theories) circulated widely but the entry uses the publicly-stated dated event per rule. No private individuals named — all named figures (Putin, Zelensky, Prigozhin) are public officials/military leaders.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's Russia-Ukraine war coverage (per user instruction). Primary source for entries Feb 24, 2022 through Feb 17, 2024 is CNN's anniversary recap 'The war in Ukraine in 12 key moments' (https://www.cnn.com/2024/02/24/europe/ukraine-war-second-anniversary-key-moments-intl), which is explicitly a curated timeline article and supplied 12 anchor dated events, plus two more referenced inline (Jan 14 2023 Dnipro strike, Oct 2023 Hroza attack). Additional contributing articles: https://www.cnn.com/2022/01/28/europe/ukraine-russia-explainer-war-threat-cmd-intl ; https://www.cnn.com/2022/02/24/europe/ukraine-russia-conflict-explainer-2-cmd-intl ; https://www.cnn.com/2022/02/24/europe/ukraine-russia-invasion-thursday-intl ; https://www.cnn.com/2022/04/06/europe/ukraine-russia-war-technology-intl-cmd ; https://www.cnn.com/europe/live-news/russia-ukraine-war-news-06-14-22 ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/02/19/europe/russia-ukraine-war-anniversary-intl-cmd ; https://www.cnn.com/europe/live-news/russia-ukraine-war-news-03-07-23 ; https://www.cnn.com/europe/live-news/russia-ukraine-war-news-07-15-23 ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/16/europe/ukraine-russia-war-frontlines-latest-intl (source for Aug 6 2024 Kursk incursion and Nov 11 2024 North Korean troops entries, both of which were also covered in CNN's standalone Kursk coverage referenced inline in that piece). Articles considered but dropped per rule 5 (out of cluster window): https://www.cnn.com/2025/03/26/europe/timeline-trumps-pledge-to-end-ukraine-war (a 2025 timeline of Trump's negotiations entirely after the cluster window) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/30/europe/russias-summer-offensive-ukraine-analysis-latam-intl (Jul 2025) ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/08/12/europe/russia-ukraine-donetsk-dobropillia-trump-putin-intl (Aug 2025). Date approximations per rule 4: Bucha (Apr 1, 2022) — CNN's 12-key-moments piece dates the heading 'April 1, 2022: Bucha atrocities' but the discovery of bodies and global revelation occurred over Apr 1-4; Apr 1 used as CNN's chosen anchor date for the section. Hroza cafe attack — CNN's piece says 'October 2023' without a specific day; Oct 5, 2023 used as the well-established date of the attack, which CNN's separate Oct 2023 coverage dated explicitly. Bakhmut fall (May 20, 2023) — CNN's piece uses this as the section header. Kupiansk recapture (Sep 10, 2022) — CNN's Nov 16, 2024 piece says it was 'liberated by the Ukrainians in September 2022'; Sep 10 used as the widely-reported date of liberation. Source disagreements: Moskva sinking — CNN explicitly notes the cause was disputed (Ukrainian missile strike vs Russian fire claim); entry captures CNN's reporting that the loss happened on Apr 14 regardless of cause. Mariupol theatre death toll given as 'roughly 300' per Ukrainian authorities, with CNN noting it could not independently verify; entry preserves CNN's reported figure per rule. Wagner mutiny (Jun 23 2023) — CNN reports Prigozhin's forces shot down Russian aircraft and killed servicemen; Russia briefly dropped charges before later events; entry captures the Jun 23 action as stated. No private individuals named.
